member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.Handlers.SQLHandlers
member.summary: Provides static helper methods for configuring and executing SQL Server bulk copy operations and related table management tasks.
member.remarks: The SQLHandlers class is intended for use with SQL Server data access scenarios that require efficient bulk data operations or temporary table management. All methods are static and require valid SQL Server connection and transaction objects. This class does not manage connection or transaction lifetimes; callers are responsible for ensuring that provided connections and transactions are open and valid for the duration of the operation.

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.SqlServerQueryBuilder
member.summary: Provides static methods for generating SQL Server query strings for common operations such as identity insert, temporary table management, and merge statements.
member.remarks: This class is intended to assist with dynamic SQL generation for SQL Server scenarios, including bulk operations and upserts. All methods return SQL statements as strings and do not execute any database commands. Callers are responsible for validating input parameters and executing the generated queries using appropriate database access methods. Thread safety is ensured as all members are static and stateless.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.SqlServerQueryBuilder.SetIdentityInsertQuery(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: This method is used to set identity on off for identity insert

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.SqlServerQueryBuilder.GetTempTableName(System.String)
member.summary: This method Get the temporary table name

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.SqlServerQueryBuilder.DropTableIfExistsQuery(System.String)
member.summary: This method is used to drop table if exists

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.SqlServerQueryBuilder.GenerateUpdateMergeQuery(System.String,System.String,System.Data.DataTable,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails)
member.summary: This method is used to generate update merge query

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.SqlServerQueryBuilder.GenerateInsertOrUpdateMergeQuery(System.String,System.String,System.Data.DataTable,Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails)
member.summary: This method is used to generate insert or update merge query

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlServer.SqlServerQueryBuilder.GenerateTemperoryTableQuery(Gurung.EfBulkOperations.TableDetails)
member.summary: This method is used to generate temperory table query

member.name: T:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.SqlServer.SqlServerDataHandler
member.summary: Provides SQL Server-specific implementations for performing bulk data operations such as insert, update, and insert-or-update using Entity Framework Core DbContext instances.
member.remarks: This class is designed to efficiently handle large-scale data modifications in SQL Server databases by leveraging optimized techniques such as transactions, temporary tables, and SQL MERGE statements. It is intended for use in scenarios where standard Entity Framework operations would be inefficient for processing large collections of entities. All operations are performed asynchronously and ensure atomicity through database transactions. The methods require a properly configured DbContext and are suitable for use in high-performance data processing applications.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.SqlServer.SqlServerDataHandler.BulkInsertAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously inserts a collection of entities into the database in bulk using the specified DbContext.
member.remarks: This method uses a database transaction to ensure that all entities are inserted as a single atomic operation. The connection is opened and closed automatically. For large collections, bulk insert can significantly improve performance compared to inserting entities individually.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to insert. Must be a class that is mapped by the DbContext.
member.param: The DbContext instance used to access the database. Must not be null and must be configured for the target entity type.
The collection of entities to insert. Must not be null and must contain at least one entity.
An optional configuration object that specifies bulk insert options, such as batch size or column mappings. If null, default settings are used.
A CancellationToken that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous bulk insert operation.
member.exception: Thrown if an error occurs during the bulk insert operation.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.SqlServer.SqlServerDataHandler.BulkUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Performs a bulk update operation on the specified entities in the database asynchronously using a temporary table and a merge statement.
member.remarks: This method uses a temporary table and a SQL MERGE statement to efficiently update multiple records in a single database operation. The operation is performed within a transaction to ensure atomicity. After completion, the temporary table is dropped and the database connection is closed. This method is suitable for scenarios where updating a large number of records individually would be inefficient.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to update. Must be a class that is mapped to a table in the provided DbContext.
member.param: The DbContext instance used to access the database and track changes.
The collection of entities to update in bulk. Each entity must correspond to a row in the target database table.
An optional configuration object that specifies settings for the bulk operation, such as batch size or column mappings. If null, default settings are used.
A cancellation token that can be used to cancel the asynchronous operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous bulk update operation.
member.exception: Thrown if an error occurs during the bulk update process.

member.name: M:Gurung.EfBulkOperations.SqlDataHandler.SqlServer.SqlServerDataHandler.BulkInsertOrUpDateAsync``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},Gurung.EfBulkOperations.BulkConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Performs a bulk insert or update operation for a collection of entities in the specified DbContext using optimized SQL Server techniques.
member.remarks: This method uses temporary tables and SQL Server MERGE statements to efficiently insert new records or update existing ones in bulk. The operation is performed within a transaction to ensure atomicity. After completion, the temporary table is dropped and the database connection is closed. This method is intended for scenarios where large numbers of entities need to be synchronized with the database efficiently.
member.typeparam: The type of the entities to insert or update. Must be a class mapped to a database table in the provided DbContext.
member.param: The DbContext instance used to access the database and track changes.
The collection of entities to be inserted or updated in bulk. Cannot be null.
An optional configuration object that specifies bulk operation settings such as batch size or column mappings. If null, default settings are used.
A token to monitor for cancellation requests. The operation is canceled if the token is triggered.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous bulk insert or update operation.
member.exception: Thrown if an error occurs during the bulk insert or update process. The inner exception contains details about the underlying failure.
